# 🚀 Enterprise Data Platform — Deployment Readiness Report

**Project:** gamma — Enterprise Data Platform (EDP)
**Review Phase:** Deployment Readiness Review (Final — Phases 1–4 Consolidated)
**Report Date:** Current
**Classification:** Internal — Engineering Confidential
**Prepared By:** Automated Architecture Review System
**Source Documents Reviewed:** 70 documents (all constraints, ADRs, design docs, incident reports, service specs, runbooks, compliance docs, infrastructure manifests, meeting notes, RFCs)

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The Enterprise Data Platform is **conditionally ready for go-live** with Wave 1 (e-commerce) workloads. The core infrastructure is sound and battle-tested, all seven constraints are architecturally satisfied, and compliance frameworks (GDPR, PCI-DSS, SOC 2) are substantially implemented. However, **six blocking items** and **eleven high-priority gaps** must be resolved before full production go-live. The most critical are: the ADR-008 retention policy decision remains unresolved (DRAFT status), the transactional outbox for payment-processor is still in-progress (directly implicated in INC-2025-456 causing $234K in duplicate charges), PII classification is only 85% complete, and the PostgreSQL connection pool will breach capacity at the Q1 2026 international expansion launch.

---

## SECTION 1: DECISION CONSISTENCY MATRIX

This matrix cross-references every major architectural decision from Phases 1–4 and verifies internal consistency. Conflicts and tensions are explicitly flagged.

### 1.1 Core Technology Decisions

| Decision ID | Decision | Status | Consistent With | Conflicts / Tensions |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **ADR-001** | Apache Kafka (Confluent 7.6), 5 brokers, KRaft mode, RF=3, min.ISR=2 | ✅ Accepted | C4 (RF≥3, min.ISR≥2), ADR-005, ADR-007, infra-k8s-manifests, ref-kafka-config | Stale topology doc (gamma-stale-topology) still shows 3 brokers, ZooKeeper, RF=2 — **must be archived/deleted** |
| **ADR-002** | Apache Avro + Confluent Schema Registry, BACKWARD compatibility default | ✅ Accepted | C1 (BACKWARD compat), C7 (automated checks), ADR-001, svc-schema-registry | Q4 review approved Protobuf exception for `edp.events.mobile.*` — ADR-002 amendment not yet formally written; spec-protobuf-proposal is PROPOSED/NOT ACCEPTED; dual-format support in edp-transform due 2026-01-15 |
| **ADR-003** | PostgreSQL 16, Patroni, PgBouncer, Flyway migrations | ✅ Accepted | spec-data-model, runbook-db-migration, design-capacity | **CRITICAL:** Q1 2026 capacity spec shows PostgreSQL connections will hit 520 vs. 500 capacity at international launch — PgBouncer increase to 750 is a P0 action item not yet completed |
| **ADR-004** | Kubernetes 1.29 (EKS), Strimzi, Istio, Argo Rollouts, Vault Agent | ✅ Accepted | infra-k8s-manifests, infra-terraform, spec-security-model | K8s manifests use `edp` namespace but ADR-004 specifies `edp-prod` — minor naming inconsistency to verify |
| **ADR-005** | Exactly-once via Kafka transactions + transactional outbox + idempotency keys | ✅ Accepted | C2 (EOS for payments), design-exactly-once, svc-consumer | **BLOCKING:** Transactional outbox for payment-processor is still "In Progress" (deadline 2026-01-15 per incident retro). INC-2025-456 and INC-2025-112 both demonstrate the current at-least-once + idempotency approach is fragile. Dedup TTL was extended to 24h (post-INC-2025-456) but ADR-005 recommends 48h minimum |
| **ADR-006** | Prometheus + Grafana + Jaeger + ELK (Loki/Tempo in manifests) | ✅ Accepted | svc-monitoring, ref-grafana-dashboards, ref-slo-dashboard | Minor: ADR-006 specifies Jaeger; manifests reference Tempo. Both are OpenTelemetry-compatible backends — functionally consistent but documentation should be reconciled |
| **ADR-007** | 48-hour Kafka event retention (with exceptions: 7d financial, indefinite changelog, 30d DLQ) | ✅ Accepted | C5 (GDPR storage limitation), compliance-gdpr, ref-kafka-config | **OPEN DECISION:** ADR-008 (72h extension) is DRAFT/NOT ACCEPTED. Architecture Board deferred final decision to 2026-01-20. INC-2025-142 (52h bug discovery) and INC-2025-003 directly motivate the change. RFC-retention-policy recommends tiered retention. **Current production config must remain 48h until ADR-008 is formally accepted.** |

### 1.2 Schema & Serialization Consistency

| Area | Decision | Consistent? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Default schema format | Avro (ADR-002) | ✅ | All server-side services use Avro |
| Mobile schema format | Protobuf exception approved (Q4 review) | ⚠️ Partial | `edp.events.mobile.*` topics use Protobuf; edp-transform dual-format support due 2026-01-15; ADR-002 amendment not yet written |
| Compatibility mode | BACKWARD default, FULL for financial (decision-log 2025-10-12) | ✅ | Consistent with C1, C7, svc-schema-registry |
| Schema naming | `com.edp.events.{domain}.{Entity}{Action}Event` | ✅ | Consistent across spec-event-schema, ADR-002, svc-schema-registry |
| Breaking change policy | 30-day deprecation, new topic required (C7, ADR-002) | ✅ | Enforced by CI pipeline and Schema Registry |
| Schema Registry HA | 3-instance deployment (svc-schema-registry) | ✅ | Addresses INC-2025-089 root cause (was single-node) |
| Schema cache in consumers | Circuit breaker + local cache (svc-ingest) | ✅ | Addresses INC-2025-089 action item #4 |

### 1.3 Security Architecture Consistency

| Area | Decision | Consistent? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Inter-broker TLS | TLS 1.3, cert-manager, 90-day rotation (spec-security-model, INC-2025-201 fix) | ✅ | Addresses INC-2025-201 root cause (manual cert management) |
| Client-broker auth | mTLS + SASL/SCRAM-512 (ADR-001, spec-security-model) | ✅ | Consistent across all service specs |
| Secret management | HashiCorp Vault, Vault Agent sidecar (ADR-004, spec-security-model) | ✅ | No secrets in ConfigMaps or env vars |
| PII encryption | Vault Transit, AES-256-GCM field-level (C3, compliance-gdpr, compliance-pci) | ⚠️ Partial | PII classification only 85% complete (Q4 review); deadline 2026-01-31 |
| Network isolation | Default-deny NetworkPolicy, Istio mTLS mesh (ADR-004, infra-k8s-manifests) | ✅ | Consistent |
| Consumer isolation | Tier 1/2/3 RFC approved 2025-09-20; Tier 3 for payments approved Q4 review | ⚠️ Partial | Tier 3 dedicated cluster for payment-processor not yet deployed (deadline 2026-02-01) |

### 1.4 Operational Consistency

| Area | Decision | Consistent? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Consumer group strategy | CooperativeSticky (ADR-005, decision-log 2025-10-20) | ✅ | Adopted after INC-2025-112; consistent across svc-consumer, ref-kafka-config |
| Offset management | Manual commit, `enable.auto.commit=false` (ADR-005) | ✅ | Consistent with INC-2025-112 and INC-2025-456 fixes |
| Idempotency TTL | 24h in Redis post-INC-2025-456 (was 1h) | ⚠️ Concern | ADR-005 and incident retro recommend ≥48h (Kafka retention window). Current 24h is still shorter than the 48h replay window — a consumer restart after 25h could still produce duplicates |
| Deployment strategy | RollingUpdate default; Blue-green (Argo Rollouts) for payment-consumer | ✅ | Consistent with ADR-004; deployment guard (max 1 pod change) added post-INC-2025-456 |
| Partition count | 12 default (decision-log 2025-10-05) | ✅ | Consistent with ADR-001, ref-kafka-config, capacity model |
| Retention (financial topics) | 7 days (ADR-007 exception, ref-kafka-config) | ✅ | Consistent with PCI-DSS Req 3.1 |
| CDC topic retention | 72h (ref-kafka-config) | ⚠️ Note | CDC topics already use 72h retention — this is inconsistent with the 48h standard in ADR-007 but aligns with RFC-retention-policy recommendation. Needs formal ADR coverage |

### 1.5 Consistency Summary

| Category | Status | Critical Issues |
|---|---|---|
| Core infrastructure | ✅ Consistent | Stale topology doc must be removed |
| Schema/serialization | ⚠️ Minor gaps | ADR-002 amendment needed for Protobuf; dual-format support pending |
| Security | ⚠️ Gaps | PII classification 85%, Tier 3 isolation not deployed |
| Exactly-once processing | ⚠️ Blocking | Transactional outbox in-progress; idempotency TTL too short |
| Retention policy | ⚠️ Open decision | ADR-008 unresolved; CDC topics already at 72h without formal ADR |
| Monitoring/observability | ✅ Consistent | Jaeger vs. Tempo naming discrepancy minor |
| Disaster recovery | ✅ Consistent | DR plan comprehensive; quarterly tests defined |

---

## SECTION 2: COMPLIANCE CHECKLIST

### 2.1 GDPR Compliance

| Requirement | Article | Control | Status | Evidence Location | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Lawfulness of processing | Art. 5.1.a | Consent tracking in customer events | ✅ Met | DataHub metadata, Customer.consent field | — |
| Purpose limitation | Art. 5.1.b | Data catalog purpose annotations | ✅ Met | DataHub | — |
| Data minimisation | Art. 5.1.c | Schema review requires PII justification | ⚠️ Partial | Schema review checklist | PII classification only 85% complete; 15% of schemas unclassified |
| Accuracy | Art. 5.1.d | Data quality rules per dataset | ✅ Met | Quality score dashboard | — |
